title: K000152956: BIG-IP strategic update: Modernizing BIG-IP TMOS and discontinuing BIG-IP Next

metadata.excerpt: F5 launched the BIG-IP Next program to modernize F5’s BIG-IP software and empower customers to adapt to the demands of the evolving application landscape. ... The program focused on delivering en...

content.sfdetails__c:
F5 launched the BIG-IP Next program to modernize F5’s BIG-IP software and empower customers to adapt to the demands of the evolving application landscape. The program focused on delivering enhanced scalability, simplified operations, and advanced automation capabilities to BIG-IP. Initially, many of the enhancements envisioned for BIG-IP Next were considered unattainable within the monolithic architecture of BIG-IP TMOS. However, recent technological advances—including cutting-edge development tools and innovative third-party software components—have demonstrated that these improvements can now be realized within the existing BIG-IP TMOS codebase.

Extensive customer feedback in recent years also highlighted various challenges associated with the adoption of BIG-IP Next, such as the need for significant solution reintegration and recertification efforts. In response, to minimize disruption for BIG-IP customers and to accelerate delivery of a solution built for the next application era, F5 has made the strategic decision to discontinue development of BIG-IP Next. F5 will instead extend and augment its existing BIG-IP TMOS software, incorporating many of the improvements originally intended for BIG-IP Next—such as greater control plane scale, accelerated software upgrades, and comprehensive automatability. These advancements began rolling out from November 2025 with the release of BIG-IP 21.0.0.

Note: The decision to discontinue BIG-IP Next does not apply to BIG-IP Next Service Proxy for Kubernetes (SPK), BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes or BIG-IP Next Cloud-Native Network Functions (CNFs). F5 will continue to develop these offerings to support F5 customers in delivering and securing modern applications.

This strategic shift allows F5 to deliver a modern, high-performance BIG-IP solution designed for the next application era while avoiding disruptions to customer operations and application traffic. The following sets of frequently asked questions provide deeper insights into these directional changes and outlines plans for both BIG-IP TMOS and BIG-IP Next.

Frequently asked questions: BIG-IP TMOS 21.x and beyond

Are there any plans to replace BIG-IP TMOS in future?

No, F5 is investing significant resources to modernize BIG-IP TMOS for the next era of applications. Looking to the future, F5 recommends BIG-IP TMOS as the F5 solution of choice for mission-critical, traditional applications deployed in any environment.

Will BIG-IP TMOS 21.x be a full-featured continuation from BIG-IP TMOS 17.x?

Yes, F5 will carry forward all functionality from BIG-IP 17.x, in addition to various new software enhancements and application delivery and security functionality, to form the foundation of BIG-IP 21.x. Capabilities carried forward include core management functionality such as iControl, TMSH, and the BIG-IP Automation Toolchain.

Are there any plans to deliver BIG-IP TMOS 18.x or 19.x?

No, BIG-IP TMOS software versions will skip from BIG-IP 17.x to BIG-IP 21.x, marking the start of the codebase modernization for BIG-IP TMOS.

What platforms will BIG-IP TMOS 21.x be supported on?

Customers will be able to deploy BIG-IP TMOS 21.x on the F5 rSeries hardware appliances and VELOS chassis systems or on BIG-IP Virtual Editions. For BIG-IP VE users, upgrades to BIG-IP 21.x will be available to those using subscription or Pay-As-You-Go (PAYG) licensing models, as well as to customers with Perpetual Version Plus V23 BIG-IP VE licenses.

What will be the upgrade process to BIG-IP TMOS 21.x?

From a process perspective, the upgrade process from BIG-IP TMOS 17.x to BIG-IP TMOS 21.x will be identical to all previous BIG-IP TMOS software upgrades. In the future, F5 plans to make it possible to perform in-place upgrades that do not require system restarts.

Does this announcement impact F5OS and its lifecycle?

No, this announcement does not impact the development or lifecycle of F5OS.

Frequently asked questions: BIG-IP Next

When will BIG-IP Next reach end of life?

The final release of BIG-IP Next (20.3.0) reached End of Software Development (EoSD) and End of Technical Support (EoTS) on April 30, 2025. As such, BIG-IP Next has already reached the end of its software lifecycle.

Will there be a BIG-IP Next 21.0 release as originally planned?

No, BIG-IP Next 20.3.0 is the final release of BIG-IP Next. F5 released BIG-IP 21.0.0 in November 2025.

What does this change mean for customers who have already deployed BIG-IP Next 20.x in production?

F5 will partner with any early adopters to create a tailored BIG-IP TMOS transition plan and ensure a prompt migration at no additional cost.

Does this announcement also impact BIG-IP Next Service Proxy for Kubernetes (SPK), BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes and BIG-IP Next Cloud-Native Network Functions (CNFs)?

No, the lifecycle and functionality of BIG-IP Next Service Proxy for Kubernetes (SPK), BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes and BIG-IP Next CNFs will not be impacted by this announcement. F5 continues to invest heavily to develop and enhance these solutions and views them as strategically important solutions for supporting modern, Kubernetes-based applications.
